title: Congo Peace May Herald $700 Million Power Deal With US Company

title_detail.value: Congo Peace May Herald $700 Million Power Deal With US Company

summary: Symbion Power LLC is proposing a 140-megawatt power plant and transmission lines along the border of Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda if the two countries can implement a recent US-backed peace agreement.

summary_detail.value: Symbion Power LLC is proposing a 140-megawatt power plant and transmission lines along the border of Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda if the two countries can implement a recent US-backed peace agreement.

author: Michael J Kavanagh

content.value: Technicians repair electrical transmission lines following power outages caused by clashes as residents begin to venture onto the streets in Goma on January 31, 2025. The Rwandan-backed armed group M23 moved south as it closed in on a key military airport in DR Congo on January 31, 2025, a day after pledging to take the capital Kinshasa and as international criticism mounted. The group's capture of most of Goma, the capital of North Kivu province, earlier in the week was a dramatic escalation in a region that has seen decades of conflict involving multiple armed groups. (Photo by Jospin Mwisha / AFP) (Photo by JOSPIN MWISHA/AFP via Getty Images) Photographer: JOSPIN MWISHA/AFP

title: Tesla Disbands Dojo Supercomputer Team, Upending AI Work

title_detail.value: Tesla Disbands Dojo Supercomputer Team, Upending AI Work

summary: Tesla Inc. is disbanding its Dojo team, upending the automaker’s effort to build an in-house supercomputer for developing driverless-vehicle technology. The team has lost about 20 workers recently to newly formed DensityAI, and remaining Dojo workers are being reassigned to other data center and compute projects within Tesla. Craig Trudell reports on Bloomberg Television.

summary_detail.value: Tesla Inc. is disbanding its Dojo team, upending the automaker’s effort to build an in-house supercomputer for developing driverless-vehicle technology. The team has lost about 20 workers recently to newly formed DensityAI, and remaining Dojo workers are being reassigned to other data center and compute projects within Tesla. Craig Trudell reports on Bloomberg Television.
